areas.title: Section 4: Detecting subscription emails programmatically
areas.notes: Verified detection.ts (lines 15-221) implements comprehensive subscription detection with confidence scoring. SPEC GAP: Code does not check Gmail category labels (category:promotions, category:updates) as required by spec Section 4 line 87. All other spec requirements met: List-Unsubscribe header with RFC 8058 one-click detection (lines 25-58), List-Id header (60-77), Precedence header (79-97), sender patterns (119-178 - 57 patterns vs 5 in spec), domain patterns (179-218 - 18 ESPs vs 3 in spec), body parsing for unsubscribe links (297-336). IMPROVEMENTS: Auto-Submitted header detection (99-117), confidence scoring (0-100 scale), mailto parsing. Existing issue #86 covers category gap.
improvements: Auto-Submitted header detection (spec omission)
57 sender patterns vs 5 in spec
18 ESP domain patterns vs 3 in spec
Confidence scoring (0-100 scale)

areas.title: Section 5: Automated unsubscription via List-Unsubscribe header
areas.notes: Verified unsubscribe.ts (lines 1-149) implements all spec requirements exactly. RFC 8058 one-click unsubscribe (13-39): POST request with Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded and body List-Unsubscribe=One-Click, matches spec lines 109-117. Mailto unsubscribe (44-65): parses mailto URL via parseMailtoUnsubscribe and sends via Gmail API, matches spec lines 119-120. HTTP GET fallback (71-110): handles GET requests with redirect detection for confirmation pages, matches spec lines 122-127 edge case handling. Body parsing for unsubscribe links (detection.ts:297-336): implements regex patterns when List-Unsubscribe header missing, matches spec line 123. Error reporting via UnsubscribeResult interface includes error messages for failures (broken/expired links), matches spec line 127. Job processing (workers.ts:27-36, jobs.ts:330-427): queues unsubscription attempts with BullMQ, records attempts to UnsubscriptionAttempt table, handles token errors. IMPROVEMENTS: Database audit trail via UnsubscriptionAttempt model, 90-day data retention (jobs.ts:333-338), specific token error handling (jobs.ts:400-423), redirect detection for manual confirmation (unsubscribe.ts:79-90).
improvements: Database audit trail via UnsubscriptionAttempt model (status tracking, error messages)
90-day data retention for unsubscription attempts (jobs.ts:333-338)
Specific token error handling with user-friendly messages (jobs.ts:400-423)
Redirect detection for confirmation pages requiring manual action (unsubscribe.ts:79-90)

areas.title: Section 3: Gmail API technical architecture
areas.notes: All spec requirements met. OAuth scopes correctly configured (auth.config.ts:48-56): gmail.readonly, gmail.modify, gmail.labels, gmail.send with full URIs. Critical OAuth params present: access_type=offline (line 46) and prompt=consent (line 45) for refresh token. Token management (auth.ts:69-128) includes auto-refresh with database persistence and InvalidTokenError handling. Bulk deletion uses batchDelete API (client.ts:304-324) with 1000 ID chunks per spec. Rate limit handling (listMessages:174-256, listHistory:371-425) uses exponential backoff with random jitter. History.list API implemented for incremental sync (371-446). IMPROVEMENT: Token encryption uses AES-256-GCM via lib/crypto.ts (stronger than spec's AES-256). IMPROVEMENT: Automatic token refresh on expiry (auth.ts:69-128).
improvements: Token encryption uses AES-256-GCM mode via lib/crypto.ts (stronger than spec's base AES-256)
Automatic token refresh with database persistence (auth.ts:69-128)
InvalidTokenError custom exception for graceful revoked token handling

areas.title: Section 6: Database schema for subscription tracking
areas.notes: Verified all 6 database models against spec (Section 6, lines 204-288). Subscription model (schema.prisma:65-86) includes all 14 required fields: id, userId, senderEmail, senderName, listUnsubscribeHeader, unsubscribeMethod, unsubscribeUrl, unsubscribeMailto, messageCount, firstSeenAt, lastSeenAt, confidenceScore, recentSubject, recentSnippet. SubscriptionPreference model (88-101) has all 7 fields. GmailSyncState model (103-116) has all 9 fields including scanStatus, scanProgress, scanTotal for UX. UnsubscriptionAttempt model (137-149) has all 8 fields for audit trail. BulkDeletionJob model (151-163) has all 9 fields for async operations. RollupSettings model (124-135) has all 7 fields with DeliverySlot enum (MORNING, AFTERNOON, EVENING). All indexes match spec recommendations. No drift found.

areas.title: Section 7: Node.js technical implementation architecture
areas.notes: Technology stack fully matches: Next.js 15.1.0, NextAuth v5, Prisma 7.7.0, BullMQ 5.74.1, googleapis 144.0.0. OAuth correctly configured with access_type=offline and prompt=consent (auth.config.ts:45-46). Token encryption uses AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2 key derivation (src/lib/crypto.ts) - actually stronger than spec's AES-256. All required queues implemented: email-scan, unsubscribe, rollup-digest. Daily digest scheduling with cron '0 8 * * *' working (src/modules/queues/queues.ts:79-95). Email monitoring via manual scans implemented. IMPROVEMENT: Code includes historyMonitorQueue for polling Gmail history API (spec listed this as future enhancement).
improvements: Token encryption uses AES-256-GCM mode (stronger than spec's AES-256)
History monitoring via historyMonitorQueue implemented (spec listed as future)

areas.title: Section 8: Google verification process and CASA compliance
areas.notes: Section 8 describes external compliance requirements (Google OAuth verification, CASA audits) rather than code functionality. Codebase appropriately documents these requirements: README.md (lines 136-168) contains comprehensive verification timeline table (4-8 weeks minimum, 3-6 months with issues), CASA Tier 2/3 costs ($500-1000/$4500+ annually), unverified app limitations (100 user limit, 7-day refresh token expiry). DEPLOYMENT.md (line 42) includes restricted scope warning. auth.config.ts (lines 48-56) correctly uses all required restricted scopes (gmail.readonly, gmail.modify, gmail.labels, gmail.send). Documentation is more comprehensive than spec with OAuth consent screen setup guidance and test user instructions. NEUTRAL: External compliance process, not code implementation.
improvements: README.md verification documentation more comprehensive than spec (includes test user guidance, OAuth consent setup)

areas.title: Section 9: Legal compliance requirements (GDPR, privacy policy)
areas.notes: Google Limited Use policy FULLY COMPLIANT: privacy page (41) includes exact disclosure text, no data selling (96), no advertising (97), no AI training (98), user deletion (settings/page.tsx:203-258, delete/route.ts). Data types listed (56-62), purpose stated (75-83), storage/duration (127-131), third parties disclosed (140-152). Disclosure appears on privacy page (39-42), signin page (118), settings (147-149), how-it-works (187). Right to erasure implemented via cascade delete in schema (User model lines 20-25, Account/Sessions/Subscription/Preference/GmailSyncState/RollupSettings all cascade). Data export API exists (export/route.ts). GDPR Rights section covers all 5 rights (158-175). SPEC GAP: No Terms of Service page (footer only links Privacy). REGRESSION: UnsubscriptionAttempt (137-149) and BulkDeletionJob (151-163) models lack onDelete Cascade - user deletion leaves audit trail data orphaned, violating GDPR right to erasure. GDPR compliance mechanisms (DPA, SCC, breach notification) documented in existing issue #92.
improvements: Data export functionality for GDPR portability (export/route.ts)
All 5 GDPR rights explicitly listed (access, rectification, erasure, portability, objection)
Limited Use disclosure appears in 4 locations (privacy, signin, settings, how-it-works)
regressions: UnsubscriptionAttempt model (schema.prisma:137-149) missing onDelete Cascade - audit data orphaned after user deletion
BulkDeletionJob model (schema.prisma:151-163) missing onDelete Cascade - job records orphaned after user deletion

areas.title: Section 10: Product architecture and feature requirements
areas.notes: Phase 1 MVP architecture FULLY COMPLIANT: Next.js 15.1.0 confirmed (package.json:44), NextAuth v5 beta (src/modules/auth/auth.config.ts), googleapis v144.0.0 (package.json:41), subscriptions module (src/modules/gmail/), PostgreSQL via Prisma 7.7.0, Redis via ioredis 5.10.1 + BullMQ 5.74.1 (src/modules/queues/queues.ts:2-77). All 5 required queues implemented: email-scan, unsubscribe, rollup-digest, bulk-delete, history-monitor. Phase 2 scale requirement neutral - workers correctly embedded in MVP (spec states Phase 2 extracts workers for scale). shadcn/ui components: 13 of 15 spec-required components present (button, card, table, dialog, dropdown-menu, select, checkbox, badge, tabs, toast, avatar, scroll-area, alert-dialog, separator, switch, radio-group). SPEC GAP: data-table and skeleton components not implemented - basic table.tsx used instead, skeleton implemented inline in dashboard/page.tsx:119-134 rather than as reusable component. Not critical for MVP.
improvements: history-monitor queue for Gmail API polling (spec listed as future enhancement)
Inline skeleton implementation in dashboard (simple, effective pattern)

areas.title: Section 2: Competitive landscape and feature gaps
areas.notes: Section 2 is informational market analysis. Competitive positioning against Unroll.me is well-implemented: homepage (page.tsx:44-46, 98-106) and how-it-works page (page.tsx:126-174) explicitly reference data selling scandal and privacy-first positioning. GDPR compliance implemented (privacy/page.tsx:158-175) with Article 17 right to erasure, data portability, and Delete Account feature. Bulk deletion fully implemented (gmail/client.ts:304-324) with 1000-message batches per spec. Responsive web design implemented (layout.tsx:25-30, viewport metadata). SPEC GAP: Pricing page linked (how-it-works/page.tsx:47-49) but /pricing route does not exist - documented in existing issue #102. NEUTRAL: No payment integration (MVP is free tier as indicated by 'Get Started Free'). NEUTRAL: No native mobile app (responsive web only).
improvements: GDPR compliance implementation exceeds spec with full rights page (Article 17, portability, access, rectification, objection)
Explicit competitive differentiation against Unroll.me on homepage and how-it-works page
Self-host option provided as additional differentiation (homepage:59-61)

areas.title: Section 1: Unroll.me functionality and limitations
areas.notes: Section 1 describes Unroll.me's actual functionality and limitations for competitive positioning. Homepage (page.tsx:99-105) and how-it-works page (page.tsx:149-173) accurately describe Unroll.me's filtering-only behavior, data selling scandal, and differentiate Prunebox as privacy-first alternative. Subscription detection (detection.ts:15-221) implements all spec methods: List-Unsubscribe header (25-58), List-Id (60-77), Precedence (79-97), sender patterns (119-178), domain patterns (179-218), recurring email detection (via messageCount grouping). True unsubscription implemented (unsubscribe.ts:1-149) with RFC 8058 one-click (13-39), mailto (44-65), http (71-110), and fallback methods. Database schema matches spec exactly (schema.prisma:65-163). IMPROVEMENTS: Auto-Submitted header detection (99-117), 57 sender patterns vs 5 in spec, 18 ESP domains vs 3 in spec, confidence scoring (0-100).
improvements: Auto-Submitted header detection for auto-generated emails (detection.ts:99-117)
57 sender patterns vs 5 in spec (newsletter@, noreply@, no-reply@, marketing@, updates@, news@, digest@, notifications@, etc.)
18 ESP domain patterns vs 3 in spec (Mailchimp, SendGrid, Constant Contact, Campaign Monitor, ConvertKit, Revue, Substack, beehiiv, etc.)
Confidence scoring system (0-100 scale) for subscription detection
